data.title: AptarGroup beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q3 outlook
data.description:
• AptarGroup press release (NYSE:ATR): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.66 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $966.01M (+6.1% Y/Y) beats by $9.15M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 13% from the prior year to $218 million.
  • Aptar currently expects earnings per share for the third quarter of 2025, excluding any restructuring expenses, changes in the fair value of equity investments and acquisition costs, to be in the range of $1.53 to $1.61 (vs. consensus of $1.65), which includes approximately 6 to 7 cents of higher legal fees associated with litigating pharma intellectual property rights. This guidance is based on an effective tax rate range of 20.5% to 22.5%, primarily due to a one-time tax benefit, with a comparable adjusted prior year effective tax rate of 23.8%. The earnings per share guidance range is based on current spot rates and a 1.15 Euro to USD exchange rate.

data.title: Gerdau GAAP EPS of R$0.43, revenue of R$17.53B
data.description:
• Gerdau press release (NYSE:GGB): Q2 GAAP EPS of R$0.43.
  • Revenue of R$17.53B (+5.5% Y/Y).
  • Steel shipments of 2.8 million tonnes in 2Q25, in line with 1Q25.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of R$2.6 billion in 2Q25, up 7% from 1Q25.
  • Investments (CAPEX) of R$1.6 billion in 2Q25, of which 80% was earmarked for Brazil.
  • Based on 2Q25 results, the Company approved the distribution of dividends in the amount of R$0.12 per share, totaling R$239.5 million. In addition, we are consistently executing our share buyback program, approved on January 20, 2025, in which we have already repurchased 43.8 million shares (GGBR3, GGBR4 and/or GGB), approximately 68% of the total program and 2.2% of the Company's outstanding shares.

data.title: France's manufacturing PMI rises marginally in July
data.description:
Manufacturing PMI in France rose/fell to 48.2 points in July from 48.1 points in June 2025.

Consensus: 48.4

New factory orders decreased at the sharpest pace since January as demand for French goods, particularly from abroad, fell sharply in July.

Commenting on the PMI data, Jonas Feldhusen, Junior Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank, said: the year. While the first half showed moderate signs of recovery, with the output index even registering growth in two successive months, the latest data point to another slowdown. Although the headline manufacturing index edged up slightly and the decline in output was only marginal, the sharp deterioration in order intakes and business expectations are causes for concern.

"This downturn in orders and expectations stands in stark contrast to the growing hopes for an economic turnaround in the first half of 2025. Recent interest rate cuts by the European Central Bank, announced defense-related investments, and regulatory easing proposals at the EU level made the soil fertile for a rebound in industrial activity. Instead, the significant drop in new orders signals growing uncertainty in the manufacturing sector. This is likely attributable both to the tense global trade environment and to the austerity agenda outlined by prime minister François Bayrou in Parliament recently. The resulting political framework is currently not conducive to investment, likely leading to cancellations or postponements of orders.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ) 

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Workiva raises 2025 revenue guidance to $873M while expanding operating margin outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Workiva (WK) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Julie Iskow, CEO, highlighted that "in Q2 of 2025, we delivered another quarter of solid financial performance, powered by the continued demand for our broad portfolio of solutions and our unified platform." She reported 23% growth in subscription revenue and 21% in total revenue, with non-GAAP operating margin exceeding guidance by 380 basis points. Iskow stated the company continues to see companies standardizing on the Workiva platform and expanding solution use across financial reporting, GRC, sustainability, and industry-specific solutions.
  • Iskow shared that "the number of contracts valued over $100,000 increased 27%. Those over $300,000 increased 37%, and contracts valued over $500,000 increased 35%, all compared to Q2 of 2024." She emphasized both additional solution sales within the existing customer base and acquiring larger new logos as key growth drivers.
  • Iskow announced a management update: "After 17 years with Workiva, Jill Klindt will be stepping down from her role as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer. Jill will continue to serve as CFO through December 2025 as we conduct a comprehensive search for our next CFO."
  • Jill E. Klindt, CFO, stated, "we had a strong Q2, generating $215 million of total revenue in the second quarter, up 21% over Q2 2024 and beating the high end of our revenue guidance by $5 million." Klindt noted that ongoing efforts to enhance operational leverage supported the quarter's non-GAAP operating margin of 3.8%.

Outlook

  • For Q3 2025, CFO Klindt provided that "we expect total revenue to range from $218 million to $220 million" and non-GAAP operating margin in the range of 7% to 8%.
  • Full year 2025 guidance was raised to "total revenue to range from $870 million to $873 million," with non-GAAP operating margin now expected to range from 7% to 7.5%. Klindt explained, "This 200 basis point improvement reflects our ongoing commitment to drive operating leverage in the business."
  • The company continues to expect subscription revenue growth will be approximately 20%, and free cash flow margin to be approximately 10.5%.
  • Iskow indicated, "the upward revision to our Q3 and full year 2025 operating margin guide reflects the continued focus on driving leverage at scale across the business and our planned progress towards achieving our 2027 margin targets."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q2 2025 was $215 million, representing 21% growth over Q2 2024, with Q2 subscription revenue at $198 million, up 23% from Q2 2024.
  • Klindt shared, "Q2 professional services revenue was $17 million, flat versus Q2 2024, with the decline in setup and consulting services offset by higher XBRL services."
  • The company ended Q2 with 6,467 customers, a growth of 320 from Q2 2024. Gross retention rate was 97%, and net retention rate was 114% for the quarter.
  • As of June 30, 2025, cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities were $814 million, an increase of $47 million over the prior quarter end. The company repurchased 132,000 shares for $10 million, with $50 million remaining under the current authorization.
  • Remaining performance obligations expected to be recognized over the next 12 months totaled $668 million, up 23% versus the prior year.

Q&A

  • Alexander James Sklar, Raymond James: Asked about capital markets contribution and FX impact on guidance. Klindt responded, "We have continued to include capital markets at a steady rate in the future. And so it could be potential upside if there's a heavier return to activity in the second half...Related to FX, we took a look at FX, and it is a part of our risk-adjusted model."
  • Sklar inquired about financial reporting upsell impact on NRR. Iskow described the company's "good, better, best model" for upselling, and Klindt added, "We did see a nice uptick in NRR during the quarter...nearing 60% of our revenue growth...came from existing customers."
  • Robert Cooney Oliver, Baird: Asked about the solutions-based model versus seat-based in the context of generative AI. Iskow stated, "We did go to that solution-based licensing model several years ago, and it has served us well for that reason, unconstrained use of the platform."
  • Steven Lester Enders, Citi: Queried about the sustainability portfolio and free cash flow. Iskow noted, "we did observe a moderation in demand in Q2 within our corporate account segment across both U.S. and Europe...sustainability is only 20% of our TAM, and it's less than 15% of our revenue." Klindt explained, "Free cash flow is a really complex metric for us because it does have a lot of factors related to the timing of cash inflows and cash outflows."
  • Dominique Calampiano Manansala, Truist: Asked about RFP activity from CFO Act agencies and M&A strategy. Iskow replied, "We're seeing early signs of -- I'll say we're having good discussions. So no comments on the actual uptick at this point."
  • Andrew Lodovico DeGasperi, BNP Paribas: Sought context on financial services wins and margin changes. Iskow said, "we're going to continue to focus on banks and the risk regulations and investments and fund reporting."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a generally positive tone, with questions focusing on growth drivers, retention, macro conditions, and operational efficiency. Several offered congratulations on results and specific performance areas.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases such as "we remain confident in our long-term prospects" and "we will be committed to bringing value to the customers using AI." Management was more direct about risk-adjusted guidance and sector-specific demand moderation compared to the prior quarter.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone is slightly more optimistic due to the raised guidance and continued demand, while acknowledging moderated sustainability demand. Analysts’ questions in both quarters focused on guidance strength and macro environment, but this quarter reflected more recognition of the company’s execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The most notable change from Q1 is the increase in full-year 2025 revenue and operating margin guidance, reflecting stronger-than-expected revenue and operational leverage. In Q1, guidance was held steady despite macro caution.
  • Net retention rate rose to 114% from 110% in Q1, supported by upsell momentum and strong multi-solution adoption.
  • The sustainability segment showed moderated demand in corporate segments in Q2, a shift from broader strength discussed in Q1. However, both quarters maintained that sustainability’s contribution to overall revenue is less than 15%.
  • There was a key management change announcement this quarter with CFO Jill Klindt’s planned departure, not present last quarter.
  • Management’s confidence in achieving 2027 and 2030 margin targets was reiterated, now with an upward revision to near-term margin outlooks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited macroeconomic uncertainty and moderated demand for sustainability solutions, particularly in the corporate segment across the U.S. and Europe.
  • Iskow said, "the demand risks in this changing market and the weighted contribution of this solution on our bookings have already been factored into the updated revenue guidance we are providing today."
  • FX fluctuations and capital markets activity were identified as variables impacting outlook, but both are included in risk-adjusted guidance.
  • Analysts raised questions about pipeline visibility, sustainability demand, and the impact of macro factors on revenue growth and retention.

Final Takeaway

Workiva delivered strong Q2 2025 results, increasing both its revenue and operating margin guidance for the year while demonstrating robust demand across its solution portfolio. Growth was powered by upsells to existing customers, new large contracts, and resilient performance in financial reporting and GRC solutions. Although the sustainability segment showed moderated demand in certain markets, this impact was risk-adjusted into the updated outlook. The company remains focused on operational leverage and profitable growth, with strong customer retention and expanding multi-solution adoption supporting confidence in long-term targets. Management also announced a planned CFO transition, ensuring continuity as the company executes its growth strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Starbucks shortlists dozen firms including Tencent, for China investment - report
data.description:
Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) has reportedly shortlisted about a dozen parties, including private equity firms and technology companies, for the second round of a process to invest in its China business.

Boyu Capital, Carlyle Group (NASDAQ:CG), EQT AB (OTCPK:EQBBF), FountainVest Partners, KKR (NYSE:KKR), Hillhouse Investment, and Primavera Capital are among the private equity firms invited to participate, along with tech giants JD.com (NASDAQ:JD) and Tencent Holdings (OTCPK:TCEHY), Bloomberg reported.

The shortlisted firms will be provided access to the coffee chain's China financials so they can evaluate and prepare bids in the coming months, the report said.

While the Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) turnaround under the direction of CEO Brian Niccol is gathering momentum, the company's fiscal third-quarter results underscore the task ahead to improve profits and restore positive same-store growth.

On the earnings call, Niccol accepted the company’s challenges and prior mismanagement but assured analysts that despite Wall Street’s reservations, the turnaround is “ahead of schedule.”
